title: sonchus vulgaris subsp. maritimus
intro: Sonchus vulgaris subsp. maritimus, also known as sea sow thistle or sea lettuce, is a subspecies of the common sow thistle plant, characterized by its ability to thrive in coastal environments. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. The health benefits of Sonchus vulgaris subsp. maritimus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical applications for skin issues and wounds.
